title: carmichaelia hollowayi
intro: Carmichaelia hollowayi, also known as the white broom or kowhai ngutu, is a species of flowering plant in the pea family Fabaceae. It is native to New Zealand and is recognized for its medicinal properties, particularly in traditional Maori medicine. The plant is valued for its ability to treat various health issues, including fever, rheumatism, and skin conditions, due to its antiseptic and anti-inflammatory properties. The herbal preparations of Carmichaelia hollowayi are often made into teas, infusions, or tinctures, and are traditionally used to promote overall health and well-being.
